channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: US downs defending champion Canada 2-1 to stay perfect at women’s hockey worlds
item.description: CESKE BUDEJOVICE, Czech Republic (AP) — The United States downed defending champion Canada 2-1 at the women’s hockey world championship on Sunday in a matchup of the game’s global powers. Lee Stecklein and Megan Keller scored for the Americans who made it three wins from three in Group A. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pacers rally from 23-point deficit in 4th quarter to defeat Cavaliers 126-118 in double overtime
item.description: Quenton Jackson scored 21 points, RayJ Dennis had seven in the second overtime and the Indiana Pacers rallied for a 126-118 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers after trailing by 23 points with 7:35 remaining in regulation.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Payton Pritchard scores 34 points in Celtics' 93-86 regular-season ending victory over Hornets
item.description: Payton Pritchard scored 34 points and the Boston Celtics swept consecutive home games against the Charlotte Hornets with a 93-86 victory despite blowing a 21-point first-half lead in a regular-season ending matchup of all reserves.
item.dc:creator: Ken Powtak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Late Van Dijk goal sees Liverpool move to within six points of the Premier League title
item.description: LIVERPOOL, England (AP) — Virgil van Dijk scored a late winner against West Ham on Sunday to move Liverpool within six points of the Premier League title.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Antetokounmpo and most other top Bucks resting for regular-season finale with Pistons
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Giannis Antetokounmpo and most of the Milwaukee Bucks’ other top players will sit out Sunday afternoon’s regular-season finale with Detroit as both teams already are locked into their respective playoff positions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Celtics star Jaylen Brown received injections in right knee, coach Joe Mazzulla says
item.description: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla has confirmed a report that Jaylen Brown received injections during the week to help his troublesome right knee that’s limited his playing time the past month.
item.dc:creator: Ken Powtak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Masters officials penalize golfer over ball move
item.description: Masters officials penalized golfer Min Woo Lee after the third round or moving his ball on the fairway. Lee was pushed toward the back of the pack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah scores four power play goals in 5-3 victory
item.description: DALLAS (AP) - Logan Cooley had a goal and two assists to lead the Utah Hockey Club to a 5-3 win against the Dallas Stars at American Airlines Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rory McIlroy chips in eagle, Bryson DeChambeau sinks deep birdie to start Masters third round with fireworks
item.description: Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau made Masters fireworks during the third round at Augusta National on Saturday, burying eagles and birdies to sit at the top of the leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Belichick reveals massive Patriots regret he made twice, in new book
item.description: Bill Belichick revealed mistakes he made during his illustrious New England Patriots tenure, and among them was passing on Lamar Jackson twice in the 2018 NFL Draft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Antonio's Stephon Castle is the rookie of the year favorite, and that has him thinking ahead
item.description: San Antonio guard Stephon Castle went through a little of everything in Year 1 of his NBA career. He's been in and out of the Spurs' starting lineup. He had Gregg Popovich coaching him for only five games. He played most of the season's second half without Victor Wembanyama. It was a roller coaster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Race fans brace the rain for 1st race of Spring Tripleheader in Bristol Motor Speedway
item.description: Despite the weather, fans still showed up to enjoy the Bristol Motor Speedway on Friday night to see their favorite NASCAR drivers.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: MLB umpire called 'perfect game' in Royals win over Twins, metrics tracking shows
item.description: The Royals defeated the Twins on Thursday. Umpire Scorecards shined a light on MLB umpire Mark Ripperger for what they concluded was a perfectly called game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 89th Masters Tournament
item.description: It's "Moving Day" at the 89th Masters Tournament at Augusta National Golf Club. The term refers to golfers who made the cut trying to get themselves into position on the leader board to win on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Fire face Inter Miami in Eastern Conference action
item.description: Inter Miami CF (4-0-2, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Fire (3-2-2, sixth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Sunday, 4:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks host the Jets following Foligno's 2-goal game
item.description: Winnipeg Jets (54-21-4, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (23-46-10, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 7 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks host the Winnipeg Jets after Nick Foligno's two-goal game against the Boston Bruins in the Blackhawks' 5-2 win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lonzo Ball meets family of meniscus donor who helped revive his NBA career
item.description: The Reinhardt family donated Alex's organs, which helped save the life of Chicago Bulls guard Lonzo Ball after he underwent cadaver meniscus transplant surgery.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Utah scores season-high 7 goals in win over Seattle
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Whether or not they make the NHL playoffs, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season with a bang.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: College athletes and San Diego students with disabilities team up for day of sports
item.description: The Inclusive Sports Foundation has provided children with special needs the opportunity to play sports with college athletes and recently stopped by the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Julian Del Gaudio, Fox 5

item.title: Cavaliers claim the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Bulls
item.description: Darius Garland scored 28 points, Evan Mobley had 21 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists and the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball: Canyon Hills 10, Southwest 7
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great Monday to play some baseball with Southwest hosting Canyon Hills. Rattlers go on to win a score fest 10-7. The Rattlers scored six in the first and held on to win, despite Southwest cutting it down to 9-7 before tacking on one more run to win by three.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Loyola University Chicago sent preservation notice tied to federally indicted ex-Michigan coach
item.description: Loyola University Chicago has received a preservation notice from attorneys representing a former student-athlete, compelling the school to preserve evidence related to the indictment of former Michigan football assistant coach Matt Weiss on unauthorized computer access and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Tickets to the Salt Lake Bees new stadium will cost you more than they used to
item.description: The Salt Lake Bees are playing their first game at the new ballpark in South Jordan tomorrow, though fans of the team might notice that prices are a little different than Smith's Ballpark.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, will face Houston team that pushes others around
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
